crime_data = read.csv("~/Downloads/ChicagoCrime.csv")
chi_2022 = read.csv("~/Downloads/Chi_2022.csv")
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)



crime_data_recent <- crime_data %>% 
  filter(Year == 2022)

crime_pivot <- crime_data_recent %>%
  group_by(`Community.Area`, `Primary.Type`) %>%
  summarise(Count = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `Primary.Type`, 
              values_from = Count,
              values_fill = 0)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Community.Area'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
crime_pivot
## # A tibble: 77 × 32
##    Community.Area ARSON ASSAULT BATTERY BURGLARY CONCEALED CARRY LICENSE VIOLA…¹
##             <int> <int>   <int>   <int>    <int>                           <int>
##  1              1     6     310     750      133                               1
##  2              2     7     290     588      164                               0
##  3              3     4     300     639      131                               2
##  4              4     3     138     300       94                               1
##  5              5     1      67     141       73                               0
##  6              6     6     299     771      203                               4
##  7              7     3     145     289      187                               1
##  8              8     5     628    1435      151                               5
##  9              9     1      20      47       26                               0
## 10             10     4      90     190       70                               0
## # ℹ 67 more rows
## # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​`CONCEALED CARRY LICENSE VIOLATION`
## # ℹ 26 more variables: `CRIMINAL DAMAGE` <int>,
## #   `CRIMINAL SEXUAL ASSAULT` <int>, `CRIMINAL TRESPASS` <int>,
## #   `DECEPTIVE PRACTICE` <int>, HOMICIDE <int>,
## #   `INTERFERENCE WITH PUBLIC OFFICER` <int>, INTIMIDATION <int>,
## #   `LIQUOR LAW VIOLATION` <int>, `MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT` <int>, …
crime_pivot <- crime_data_recent %>%
  group_by(`Community.Area`, `Primary.Type`) %>%
  summarise(Count = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `Primary.Type`, 
              values_from = Count,
              values_fill = 0)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Community.Area'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
crime_pivot
## # A tibble: 77 × 32
##    Community.Area ARSON ASSAULT BATTERY BURGLARY CONCEALED CARRY LICENSE VIOLA…¹
##             <int> <int>   <int>   <int>    <int>                           <int>
##  1              1     6     310     750      133                               1
##  2              2     7     290     588      164                               0
##  3              3     4     300     639      131                               2
##  4              4     3     138     300       94                               1
##  5              5     1      67     141       73                               0
##  6              6     6     299     771      203                               4
##  7              7     3     145     289      187                               1
##  8              8     5     628    1435      151                               5
##  9              9     1      20      47       26                               0
## 10             10     4      90     190       70                               0
## # ℹ 67 more rows
## # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​`CONCEALED CARRY LICENSE VIOLATION`
## # ℹ 26 more variables: `CRIMINAL DAMAGE` <int>,
## #   `CRIMINAL SEXUAL ASSAULT` <int>, `CRIMINAL TRESPASS` <int>,
## #   `DECEPTIVE PRACTICE` <int>, HOMICIDE <int>,
## #   `INTERFERENCE WITH PUBLIC OFFICER` <int>, INTIMIDATION <int>,
## #   `LIQUOR LAW VIOLATION` <int>, `MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT` <int>, …
crime_pivot2 <- crime_data_recent %>%
  group_by(`Community.Area`, `Primary.Type`) %>%
  summarise(Count = n())
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Community.Area'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
ggplot(crime_pivot2, aes(x = Community.Area, y = Count)) + geom_col() + xlab("Community Area") + ylab("Crime Count")

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

income = chi_2022 %>%
  summarise(GEOID, GEOG , INC_LT_25K, INC_25_50K, INC_50_75K, INC_75_100K, INC_100_150K, INC_GT_150, MEDINC, INCPERCAP, HT_Cost_Typical, HT_Cost_Mod, H_Cost_Typical,
H_Cost_Mod, T_Cost_Typical, T_Cost_Mod)
## Warning: Returning more (or less) than 1 row per `summarise()` group was deprecated in
## dplyr 1.1.0.
## ℹ Please use `reframe()` instead.
## ℹ When switching from `summarise()` to `reframe()`, remember that `reframe()`
##   always returns an ungrouped data frame and adjust accordingly.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
income

## 77             NA         NA             NA         NA
ggplot(income, aes(x = GEOID, y = MEDINC)) + geom_col()

crime_pivot <- crime_data_recent %>%
  group_by(`Community.Area`, `Primary.Type`) %>%
  summarise(Count = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `Primary.Type`, 
              values_from = Count,
              values_fill = 0)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Community.Area'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
total_crime_list = crime_pivot %>%
  mutate(total_crime = rowSums(.)) %>%
  summarise(Community.Area, total_crime)
## Warning: Returning more (or less) than 1 row per `summarise()` group was deprecated in
## dplyr 1.1.0.
## ℹ Please use `reframe()` instead.
## ℹ When switching from `summarise()` to `reframe()`, remember that `reframe()`
##   always returns an ungrouped data frame and adjust accordingly.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
income_list = chi_2022 %>%
  rename("Community.Area" = GEOID) %>%
  summarise(Community.Area, GEOG,TOT_POP, MEDINC, INCPERCAP)
## Warning: Returning more (or less) than 1 row per `summarise()` group was deprecated in
## dplyr 1.1.0.
## ℹ Please use `reframe()` instead.
## ℹ When switching from `summarise()` to `reframe()`, remember that `reframe()`
##   always returns an ungrouped data frame and adjust accordingly.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
sample_data = income_list %>%
  full_join(total_crime_list, by = "Community.Area") %>%
  mutate(crime_rate = (total_crime / TOT_POP))

total_crime_list

##    Community.Area total_crime

##  6              6        5863

##  8              8       10625
##  9              9         312
## 10             10        1280
## # ℹ 67 more rows
income_list

## 16             66           Chicago Lawn  51243.000  36277.84  17620.09
## 17             64               Clearing  24382.000  58168.13  27582.04

## 77        3279 0.14572038
ggplot(sample_data, aes(x = INCPERCAP, y = crime_rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(sample_data, aes(x = MEDINC, y = crime_rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

sample_sum = sample_data %>% 
  summarize(across(everything(), list(mean = mean, sd = sd)),
            n = n(),
            r = cor(crime_rate, INCPERCAP)) %>% 
  relocate(n)
## Warning: There were 2 warnings in `summarize()`.
## The first warning was:
## ℹ In argument: `across(everything(), list(mean = mean, sd = sd))`.
## Caused by warning in `mean.default()`:
## ! argument is not numeric or logical: returning NA
## ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 1 remaining warning.
df_lm = lm(crime_rate ~ INCPERCAP, data = sample_data)
cf = coef(df_lm)
cf
##   (Intercept)     INCPERCAP 
##  1.311743e-01 -8.742145e-07
summary(df_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = crime_rate ~ INCPERCAP, data = sample_data)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.06440 -0.04938 -0.01586  0.03400  0.18677 
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.312e-01  1.351e-02   9.708 6.77e-15 ***
## INCPERCAP   -8.742e-07  3.408e-07  -2.565   0.0123 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.05944 on 75 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.08067,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.06842 
## F-statistic: 6.582 on 1 and 75 DF,  p-value: 0.0123
cf
##   (Intercept)     INCPERCAP 
##  1.311743e-01 -8.742145e-07
ci = 1.311743e-01 + c(-1,1) * qt(0.975, 75) * -8.742145e-07
ci
## [1] 0.1311760 0.1311726
b = 1.311743e-01
t = b / 0.05944 
t
## [1] 2.206835
pt(t, df = 75, lower.tail = TRUE)
## [1] 0.9848072
race = income = chi_2022 %>%
  summarise(GEOID, GEOG , TOT_POP, WHITE, HISP, BLACK, ASIAN,OTHER)
## Warning: Returning more (or less) than 1 row per `summarise()` group was deprecated in
## dplyr 1.1.0.
## ℹ Please use `reframe()` instead.
## ℹ When switching from `summarise()` to `reframe()`, remember that `reframe()`
##   always returns an ungrouped data frame and adjust accordingly.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
race_sample = race %>%
  rename("Community.Area" = GEOID) %>%
  full_join(total_crime_list, by = "Community.Area") %>%
  mutate(P_WHITE = WHITE / TOT_POP) %>%
  mutate(P_HISP = HISP / TOT_POP) %>%
  mutate(P_BLACK = BLACK / TOT_POP) %>%
  mutate(P_ASIAN = ASIAN / TOT_POP) %>%
  mutate(P_OTHER = OTHER / TOT_POP) %>%
  mutate(crime_rate = (total_crime / TOT_POP))

race_sample

ggplot(race_sample, aes(x = P_WHITE, y = crime_rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(race_sample, aes(x = P_HISP, y = crime_rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(race_sample, aes(x = P_BLACK, y = crime_rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(race_sample, aes(x = P_ASIAN, y = crime_rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(race_sample, aes(x = P_OTHER, y = crime_rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

df_lm_MODEL = lm(crime_rate ~ P_WHITE + P_HISP + P_BLACK + P_ASIAN + P_OTHER, data = race_sample)
summary(df_lm_MODEL)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = crime_rate ~ P_WHITE + P_HISP + P_BLACK + P_ASIAN + 
##     P_OTHER, data = race_sample)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.06574 -0.02026 -0.00537  0.01163  0.13989 
## 
## Coefficients: (1 not defined because of singularities)
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)  -0.1876     0.3753  -0.500    0.619
## P_WHITE       0.2460     0.3948   0.623    0.535
## P_HISP        0.2357     0.3750   0.629    0.532
## P_BLACK       0.3737     0.3834   0.975    0.333
## P_ASIAN       0.3079     0.3844   0.801    0.426
## P_OTHER           NA         NA      NA       NA
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.03791 on 72 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.6409, Adjusted R-squared:  0.6209 
## F-statistic: 32.12 on 4 and 72 DF,  p-value: 2.344e-15

Ω

df_lm_WHITE = lm(crime_rate ~ P_WHITE, data = race_sample)
summary(df_lm_WHITE)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = crime_rate ~ P_WHITE, data = race_sample)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.076889 -0.031137 -0.007961  0.019057  0.174343 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.13721    0.00860  15.954  < 2e-16 ***
## P_WHITE     -0.12911    0.02247  -5.746 1.85e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.05165 on 75 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3057, Adjusted R-squared:  0.2964 
## F-statistic: 33.02 on 1 and 75 DF,  p-value: 1.851e-07
cf = coef(df_lm_WHITE)
cf
## (Intercept)     P_WHITE 
##   0.1372094  -0.1291097
b = -0.1291097

ci = b + c(-1,1) * qt(0.975, 75) * 0.05165
ci
## [1] -0.23200178 -0.02621762
t = b / 0.05165
t
## [1] -2.499704
pt(t, df = 75, lower.tail = TRUE)
## [1] 0.007307794
cor.test(race_sample$P_WHITE, race_sample$crime_rate)
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  race_sample$P_WHITE and race_sample$crime_rate
## t = -5.746, df = 75, p-value = 1.851e-07
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.6912467 -0.3753681
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## -0.552865
df_lm_HISP = lm(crime_rate ~ P_HISP, data = race_sample)
summary(df_lm_HISP)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = crime_rate ~ P_HISP, data = race_sample)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.091081 -0.035936  0.004311  0.027661  0.181927 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.130341   0.008543  15.257  < 2e-16 ***
## P_HISP      -0.110437   0.022480  -4.913 5.13e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.05392 on 75 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.2435, Adjusted R-squared:  0.2334 
## F-statistic: 24.13 on 1 and 75 DF,  p-value: 5.129e-06
cf = coef(df_lm_HISP)
cf
## (Intercept)      P_HISP 
##   0.1303408  -0.1104367
b = -0.1104367

ci = b + c(-1,1) * qt(0.975, 75) * 0.05392
ci
## [1] -0.217850848 -0.003022552
t = b / 0.05392
t
## [1] -2.048158
pt(t, df = 75, lower.tail = TRUE)
## [1] 0.02202249
cor.test(race_sample$P_HISP, race_sample$crime_rate)
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  race_sample$P_HISP and race_sample$crime_rate
## t = -4.9127, df = 75, p-value = 5.129e-06
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.6459958 -0.3029038
## sample estimates:
##        cor 
## -0.4934078
df_lm_BLACK = lm(crime_rate ~ P_BLACK, data = race_sample)
summary(df_lm_BLACK)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = crime_rate ~ P_BLACK, data = race_sample)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.063599 -0.019591 -0.006965  0.013903  0.142070 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.054520   0.005992   9.099 9.67e-14 ***
## P_BLACK     0.126335   0.011256  11.224  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.03787 on 75 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.6268, Adjusted R-squared:  0.6218 
## F-statistic:   126 on 1 and 75 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
cf = coef(df_lm_BLACK)
cf
## (Intercept)     P_BLACK 
##  0.05452007  0.12633533
b = 0.12633533 

ci = b + c(-1,1) * qt(0.975, 75) * 0.6268
ci
## [1] -1.122314  1.374985
t = b / 0.6268
t
## [1] 0.201556
pt(t, df = 75, lower.tail = TRUE)
## [1] 0.5795953
cor.test(race_sample$P_BLACK, race_sample$crime_rate)
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  race_sample$P_BLACK and race_sample$crime_rate
## t = 11.224, df = 75, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.6901174 0.8627141
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.7917189
df_lm_ASIAN = lm(crime_rate ~ P_ASIAN, data = race_sample)
summary(df_lm_ASIAN)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = crime_rate ~ P_ASIAN, data = race_sample)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.07737 -0.04974 -0.01366  0.03990  0.19688 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.108727   0.008065  13.481   <2e-16 ***
## P_ASIAN     -0.116943   0.064351  -1.817   0.0732 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.06067 on 75 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.04218,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.0294 
## F-statistic: 3.302 on 1 and 75 DF,  p-value: 0.07317
cf = coef(df_lm_ASIAN)
cf
## (Intercept)     P_ASIAN 
##   0.1087270  -0.1169435
b = -0.1169435

ci = b + c(-1,1) * qt(0.975, 75) * -0.1169435
ci
## [1]  0.1160199 -0.3499069
t = b / 0.04218
t
## [1] -2.772487
pt(t, df = 75, lower.tail = TRUE)
## [1] 0.00350665
cor.test(race_sample$P_ASIAN, race_sample$crime_rate)
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  race_sample$P_ASIAN and race_sample$crime_rate
## t = -1.8173, df = 75, p-value = 0.07317
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.41046599  0.01950839
## sample estimates:
##        cor 
## -0.2053678
df_lm_OTHER = lm(crime_rate ~ P_OTHER, data = race_sample)
summary(df_lm_OTHER)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = crime_rate ~ P_OTHER, data = race_sample)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.07033 -0.05135 -0.01504  0.04027  0.19961 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.11195    0.01272   8.804 3.52e-13 ***
## P_OTHER     -0.46761    0.46043  -1.016    0.313    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.06157 on 75 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.01357,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.0004133 
## F-statistic: 1.031 on 1 and 75 DF,  p-value: 0.3131
cf = coef(df_lm_OTHER)
cf
## (Intercept)     P_OTHER 
##   0.1119516  -0.4676100
b = -0.4676100 

ci = b + c(-1,1) * qt(0.975, 75) * 0.06157
ci
## [1] -0.5902637 -0.3449563
t = b / 0.06157
t
## [1] -7.59477
pt(t, df = 75, lower.tail = TRUE)
## [1] 3.509885e-11
cor.test(race_sample$P_OTHER, race_sample$crime_rate)
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  race_sample$P_OTHER and race_sample$crime_rate
## t = -1.0156, df = 75, p-value = 0.3131
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.3317955  0.1103862
## sample estimates:
##        cor 
## -0.1164723
